summary: PR #2419 proposed adding two Transmuter helper scripts and intentionally changed ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds as a demo target. The bot reported two broken matches in main/melee/if/iftime, and a maintainer asked for resubmission when the tooling was more complete and better explained. The PR was closed without merge evidence.

key_files.role: New helper script to generate a preprocessed .ctx file with ninja, copy it to .ctx.c, locate the Transmuter CLI, and print a suggested transmuter match command.
evidence_refs: pr_body: prepare command example uses src/melee/if/iftime.c and ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds

key_files.role: New compile wrapper intended to replicate the mwcc_sjis ninja rule for compiling a single preprocessed TU through sjiswrap and mwcceppc.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: script comments state it replicates mwcc_sjis for a ctx-style source file

key_files.role: Demo code target intentionally perturbed in ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds by swapping the local declaration order of seconds and centiseconds.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds moves gm_8016AF0C call after gm_8016AEEC call
pr_body: author says they purposely break ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds to demo Transmuter

what_changed: Added tools/transmuter-prepare.sh, which validates a source path and function name, maps src/... to build/GALE01 paths, requires the target object to exist, runs ninja for the .ctx file, copies it to .ctx.c, and prints a transmuter match invocation.

Added tools/transmuter-compile.sh, which validates input/output arguments, chooses wibo or wine as a PE loader, sets MWCC flags for GC/1.2.5n, and invokes sjiswrap.exe plus mwcceppc.exe on a single input C file.
Changed src/melee/if/iftime.c in ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds by reordering calls to gm_8016AEEC and gm_8016AF0C; the PR body states this was an intentional break for demonstration.
The automated report recorded matched-code regression of 66.29% (-0.01%, -260 bytes) and broken matches for ifTime_UpdateCountdown and ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds.

smart_moves.text: The prepare script tried to make the Transmuter workflow reproducible by deriving ctx, ctx.c, and target object paths from the source path and printing the exact match command.
The compile wrapper kept compiler invocation details in one script passed through Transmuter's --compiler hook instead of embedding the full mwcc command directly in the PR body.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: tools/transmuter-compile.sh CFLAGS and exec sjiswrap/mwcceppc

decomp_lessons.lesson: Tooling-demo PRs that intentionally break a matched function need especially clear scope and usage documentation before they are reviewable.
evidence_refs: pr_body: author says ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds was purposely broken to demo Transmuter
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reports broken matches
issue_comment ribbanya: resubmit when more complete with clearer usage explanation

decomp_lessons.lesson: A transmutation or permutation workflow should be separated from lasting source changes unless the PR also restores or proves the code match.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report shows ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds after 79.00% and ifTime_UpdateCountdown after 85.67%
issue_comment ribbanya: asks for resubmission in a more complete state

naming_conventions.name: ctx / ctx.c
naming_conventions.convention: The proposed tooling used .ctx for ninja-generated preprocessed TU output and copied it to .ctx.c so both MWCC and Transmuter accept the extension.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: tools/transmuter-prepare.sh comments and CTX/CTX_C variables

naming_conventions.name: TRANSMUTER_BIN
naming_conventions.convention: The prepare script allowed a TRANSMUTER environment variable to override the Transmuter CLI path.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Generate a single-TU preprocessed source with ninja, copy it from .ctx to .ctx.c, and use it as Transmuter's input.
Invoke Transmuter with a target object, function name, cwd, and compiler wrapper that accepts {{inputPath}} and {{outputPath}}.

Use --isolate to strip non-target, non-inline function bodies before the search; the script claims this can reduce iterations in practice, but the PR does not provide verifier evidence beyond the script comment.
For Linux, the compile wrapper tries build/tools/wibo, then PATH wibo, then wine, matching the script's stated intent to follow Melee configure.py behavior.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: tools/transmuter-compile.sh Linux case selects WRAPPER

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.feedback: Automated report found a matched-code regression: 66.29% total matched code, -260 bytes, with broken matches in ifTime_UpdateCountdown and ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Requested resubmission when the work is in a more complete state and has a clearer explanation of usage.

follow_up_queries: PR 2419 resubmit complete state clearer explanation

evidence_refs: raw_diff: full diff for three changed files
human_pr_text: PR body and maintainer issue comment
text_corpus: bot report and issue comments
notes: Diff and changed-file metadata are complete for the slice.
No review comments or formal reviews are present.
PR was closed with merged_at null; do not treat the tooling or tactics as accepted project practice.
The author explicitly states the iftime.c change intentionally breaks the function, and the bot report confirms broken matches.
The script comment claims --isolate makes compiles faster and search converge in fewer iterations, but the slice provides no independent benchmark or validation evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: Document expectations for tool-assisted matching demo PRs
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2419 suggests that a tooling-demo PR which intentionally changes matched code should include complete usage documentation and avoid leaving the demo target broken; a maintainer asked for resubmission in a more complete state with clearer explanation.
evidence_refs: pr_body: author says they purposely break ifTime_GetCountdownSeconds to demo Transmuter
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reports broken matches
issue_comment ribbanya: Please resubmit this when it's in a more complete state and with a clearer explanation of its usage.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential contributor-guidance update; not an accepted decomp standard because the PR was closed unmerged and reviewer feedback asked for resubmission.

source_update_candidates.title: Evaluate whether Transmuter helper scripts belong in project tooling
source_update_candidates.text: The PR proposed helper scripts for preparing .ctx.c files and compiling Transmuter candidates through MWCC. If similar tooling is revisited, curator/reviewer should compare against current build tooling and require validation that it does not regress matched code.
source_update_candidates.reason: The scripts are concrete proposed tooling, but the PR did not merge and available feedback says it was incomplete.

rejection_notes: Do not promote Transmuter usage, --isolate, --no-reduce, or the compile wrapper as accepted project standards from this PR alone.
Do not treat the iftime.c declaration-order change as a successful matching tactic; the PR body says it intentionally broke the function and the bot reported regressions.
Do not infer reviewer rejection of Transmuter itself; the loaded comment only requests a more complete resubmission with clearer usage.
